shots.prompt: Pre-dawn blue light in the cabin. Theo, oddly calm now, films himself bundling into his parka. Theo, quiet: "Last entry. I think. I keep hearing it — like it's asking. And I think I want to know." Realistic handheld found-footage, soft grain, screen glow.

POV phone footage, Theo walking out across the deep snow toward the frozen lake, breath fogging, the deep hum swelling louder. Boots crunch. Theo, behind camera: "It's waiting at the lake. It's been waiting the whole time." Cold blue dawn, handheld shake, photoreal.

The frozen lake revealed: a vast geometric pattern glowing bright beneath the ice, the tall figure standing at its center as light pours up around it. Theo, voice soft with wonder: "...it's beautiful. I don't know why I'm not scared anymore." Photoreal found-footage, overwhelming light, no music score.

Theo steps out onto the glowing ice toward the figure, the phone shaking, white light washing the frame. The radio in his pocket plays the steady signal pulse. Theo: "Tell them — tell whoever finds this — I went because I wanted to." Handheld, realistic, building to blinding light.

The light becomes blinding; the phone camera overexposes to pure white, the audio peaking into a single clean signal tone. For one instant the tall figure is right in front of the lens. The frame whites out completely. Authentic camera-overload found-footage look.

The phone lies face-up in the snow, screen cracked, still recording. Silence, then wind. Far above the frozen lake a vast light lifts off into the sky and is gone. The corner timestamp glitches and the recording cuts to harsh static. The end. Found-footage realism, ambiguous and haunting, no music.
